rawtext.text:
Mind the sparks. I'm Brenna — I keep this village in nails, hinges, and the occasional sword nobody hopes to use.

You're the one they're calling the Awoken? Hmph. Prophecies don't hold a shield wall. §oSteel§r does. But my forge is cold more days than not... unless you can feed it.

I was an armorer in the lowland wars. Forged blades for men who broke them on each other. When my company was routed, I walked north until the fighting was a rumor, and Elderfall took me in without asking what my hammer had made.

Here's the thing no one tells you about the ruins: iron pulled from this soil §osings§r on the anvil. High and clear, like a struck bell. My grandmother would've called it blessed. I call it good ore. Either way — what I forge from it does not miss.

Two jobs, if your spine's straight enough.

§6Embers of the Forge§r — bring me §f16 iron ingots§r and I'll get this forge roaring again. The village pays in emeralds; I pay in gratitude and free repairs.

§6Hold the Line§r — once the forge burns, I need proof the Corrupted can bleed. Put down §f10 Corrupted Raiders§r and the first blade off my anvil is yours: the §bElderfall Defender's Blade§r, sung from ruin-iron.
